prompt: Who scored the winning goal in the 2010 UEFA Champions League Final?
explanation: Diego Milito scored twice as Inter Milan beat Bayern Munich 2-0.

prompt: Who scored the winning goal in the 2014 FIFA World Cup Final?
explanation: Mario Gotze scored in extra time to give Germany a 1-0 win over Argentina.

prompt: Who scored the winning goal in the 2016 UEFA Euro Final for Portugal?
explanation: Eder scored in extra time to give Portugal a 1-0 win over France.

prompt: Who was named the best player (Golden Ball) of the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball as the tournament's outstanding player.

prompt: Who won the Golden Boot as top scorer at the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Kylian Mbappe scored 8 goals, including a hat-trick in the final, to win the Golden Boot.

prompt: Which club did Zinedine Zidane join for a then-world record transfer fee in 2001?
explanation: Real Madrid signed Zidane from Juventus for a then-world record fee in 2001.

prompt: Who managed Leicester City to their shock Premier League title in 2016?
explanation: Claudio Ranieri led Leicester City to a famous title win at odds of 5000-1.

prompt: Which club won the first-ever UEFA Champions League (rebranded from European Cup) in 1993?
explanation: Marseille won the 1993 final, the first played under the Champions League name.

prompt: Who is the all-time top scorer in Premier League history?
explanation: Alan Shearer scored 260 Premier League goals, a record until Harry Kane later surpassed it in 2025.

prompt: Which country did Luka Modric captain to the 2018 World Cup final?
explanation: Modric captained Croatia to a runner-up finish in 2018 and won the Golden Ball.

prompt: Which manager led Barcelona's dominant 'tiki-taka' era starting in 2008?
explanation: Pep Guardiola took over Barcelona in 2008 and led their historic treble-winning season in 2009.

prompt: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal for Argentina in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final?
explanation: Maradona punched the ball into the net against England, a goal that was wrongly allowed.

prompt: Which club did Thierry Henry play for during his most prolific Premier League years?
explanation: Henry became Arsenal's all-time top scorer during his years there from 1999-2007.

prompt: Which country eliminated Brazil 7-1 in the 2014 World Cup semi-final?
explanation: Germany famously thrashed hosts Brazil 7-1 in the semi-final in Belo Horizonte.

prompt: Who won the UEFA Champions League with three different clubs?
explanation: Clarence Seedorf won the Champions League with Ajax, Real Madrid and AC Milan.

prompt: Which club did Jurgen Klopp manage to Champions League glory in 2019?
explanation: Klopp led Liverpool to a 2-0 win over Tottenham in the 2019 Champions League final.

prompt: Who scored a famous long-range volley in the 2002 Champions League final for Real Madrid?
explanation: Zidane's stunning left-footed volley won Real Madrid the 2002 final against Leverkusen.

prompt: Which country won the 2019 Copa America on home soil?
explanation: Brazil won the 2019 Copa America as hosts, beating Peru 3-1 in the final.

prompt: Who won the 2021 Copa America final between Argentina and Brazil?
explanation: Argentina beat Brazil 1-0 at the Maracana, Messi's first major title with Argentina.

prompt: Which player scored four goals in a single World Cup match for France in 1958?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored a record 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup, including four in one match.

prompt: Which team eliminated defending champions Germany in the group stage of the 2018 World Cup?
explanation: Germany finished bottom of their group behind Mexico and South Korea in 2018.

prompt: Which manager guided Italy to the Euro 2020/2021 title?
explanation: Roberto Mancini led Italy to a shock Euro title, beating England on penalties.

prompt: Who was the top scorer of the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: England's Harry Kane won the Golden Boot with 6 goals at the 2018 World Cup.

prompt: Which club did Ronaldinho join in 2003, becoming a global superstar?
explanation: Ronaldinho joined Barcelona in 2003 and won the Ballon d'Or in 2005.

prompt: What was notable about the 2014 World Cup semi-final between Brazil and Germany?
explanation: Germany humiliated hosts Brazil 7-1, one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history.

prompt: Which player won the Champions League with Manchester United in 1999 with a last-minute winner in the final?
explanation: Solskjaer scored a stoppage-time winner as United beat Bayern Munich 2-1 in 1999.

prompt: Which country did Roger Milla represent, becoming an icon of the 1990 World Cup?
explanation: Cameroon's Roger Milla became a World Cup icon with his corner flag celebrations in 1990.

prompt: Which manager took Leicester City, then later led Chelsea, and previously managed Italy's national team?
explanation: Claudio Ranieri has managed Chelsea, various Italian clubs, and famously Leicester City.

prompt: Who scored the decisive penalty for Argentina in the 2022 World Cup Final shootout?
explanation: Gonzalo Montiel scored the winning penalty to seal Argentina's World Cup victory.

statement: Leicester City won the Premier League title in the 2015-16 season.
explanation: Leicester's title win at 5000-1 odds is considered one of sport's greatest upsets.

statement: Germany was eliminated in the group stage of the 2018 World Cup as defending champions.
explanation: Germany finished bottom of their group, a shock exit as reigning champions.

statement: Zinedine Zidane won the World Cup as both a player and a manager.
explanation: Zidane won as a player in 1998 and as France's manager in 2018.

statement: Roberto Mancini managed Italy to the Euro 2020 title.
explanation: Mancini led Italy to victory at Euro 2020 (played in 2021), beating England on penalties.

statement: Just Fontaine's record of 13 goals in a single World Cup, set in 1958, still stands.
explanation: No player has surpassed Fontaine's 13-goal tally from the 1958 World Cup.

statement: Argentina beat Brazil in the final of the 2021 Copa America.
explanation: Argentina won 1-0 at the Maracana, Messi's first senior title with the national team.

statement: The 2010 Champions League final between Inter Milan and Bayern Munich ended 2-0.
explanation: Diego Milito scored twice as Inter completed a historic treble.

statement: Harry Kane won the Golden Boot at the 2018 World Cup.
explanation: Kane scored 6 goals to top the scoring charts in Russia 2018.

statement: Clarence Seedorf is the only player to win the Champions League with three different clubs.
explanation: Seedorf won it with Ajax, Real Madrid and AC Milan — a unique achievement.

statement: Cameroon reached the quarter-finals of the 1990 World Cup.
explanation: Cameroon became the first African team to reach the World Cup quarter-finals, in 1990.

statement: Manchester United beat Bayern Munich in the 1999 Champions League final with two stoppage-time goals.
explanation: Sheringham and Solskjaer scored in stoppage time to complete a dramatic comeback.

statement: Mario Gotze scored Germany's winning goal in the 2014 World Cup final in normal time.
explanation: Gotze's winner came in extra time, not normal time, as the match was 0-0 after 90 minutes.

statement: Diego Maradona's 'Hand of God' goal came in the 1986 World Cup final.
explanation: That goal came in the quarter-final against England, not the final.

statement: Pep Guardiola won a treble in his first full season as Barcelona manager.
explanation: Guardiola's Barcelona won La Liga, the Copa del Rey and the Champions League in 2008-09.

statement: Luka Modric won the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup.
explanation: Modric was named the tournament's best player despite Croatia losing the final.
Goals from Salah and Origi gave Liverpool their sixth European Cup.
Germany demolished hosts Brazil 7-1 in one of football's biggest ever shocks.
The match finished 1-1 after 90 minutes and extra time, with Italy winning on penalties.
Angel Di Maria scored the only goal as Argentina won at the Maracana.
United scored twice in stoppage time to complete a treble-winning season.
Liverpool came back from 3-0 down at half-time to draw 3-3, then won on penalties.
The match finished 1-1 after extra time; Italy won on penalties.
Eder's extra-time strike won Portugal their first European title.

clues: Croatian midfielder
Won the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup
Long-time Real Madrid player
Known for his passing range despite his small stature
explanation: Modric was named the best player of the 2018 World Cup despite Croatia's final loss.

clues: Croatian striker
Scored a famous corner-flag celebration goal in 1990
Became a global icon of African football despite representing Cameroon
One of the oldest World Cup goal scorers ever
explanation: Roger Milla's celebrations at the 1990 World Cup made him a global icon, though he is Cameroonian, not Croatian.

clues: German striker
Holds the record for most World Cup goals ever (16)
Played for Bayern Munich and other German clubs
Nicknamed for his diving header celebrations
explanation: Klose's 16 World Cup goals across four tournaments is an all-time record.

clues: Brazilian playmaker
Won the 2005 Ballon d'Or
Famous smile and flair
Later played for AC Milan after Barcelona
explanation: Ronaldinho won the 2005 Ballon d'Or during his peak years at Barcelona.

clues: English striker
Became Premier League's all-time top scorer in 2025
Long-time Tottenham captain before joining Bayern Munich
Also England's all-time top scorer
explanation: Kane surpassed Alan Shearer's Premier League scoring record and captains England.

clues: German manager
Led Borussia Dortmund to a Champions League final in 2013
Later managed Liverpool to Champions League glory in 2019
Known for his 'heavy metal football' style
explanation: Klopp won the Champions League with Liverpool in 2019 after success at Dortmund.

clues: French forward
Scored 13 goals at a single World Cup, a record that still stands
Played at the 1958 World Cup
Teammate of Raymond Kopa
explanation: Fontaine's 13-goal haul at the 1958 World Cup remains an untouched record.

clues: Spanish manager
Won a historic treble with Barcelona in his first full season
Later managed Bayern Munich and Manchester City
Pioneer of modern possession-based football
explanation: Guardiola's Barcelona won six trophies in the 2008-09 season.

prompt: Order these Champions League winning clubs chronologically (earliest first).
events: Marseille wins the first Champions League (1993)
explanation: Order: Marseille 1993, Manchester United 1999, Barcelona 2009, Liverpool 2019.

prompt: Order these events in Zinedine Zidane's career chronologically (earliest first).
events: Zidane joins Real Madrid for a world record fee (2001)
Zidane scores a famous volley in the Champions League final (2002)
Zidane retires as a player after the World Cup final headbutt (2006)
Zidane wins the World Cup as France's manager (2018)
explanation: Order: Real Madrid transfer 2001, Champions League volley 2002, retirement 2006, managerial World Cup win 2018.

prompt: Order these Euro tournament winners chronologically (earliest first).
events: Greece wins a shock Euro title (2004)
Portugal wins their first Euro title (2016)
explanation: Order: Greece 2004, Spain 2008, Portugal 2016, Italy 2020/2021.

prompt: Order these events in Lionel Messi's trophy history chronologically (earliest first).
events: Messi wins his first Champions League with Barcelona (2006 season, awarded 2006)
Messi wins his first Ballon d'Or (2009)
Messi wins the Copa America with Argentina (2021)
Messi wins the World Cup with Argentina (2022)
explanation: Order: first Champions League title 2006, first Ballon d'Or 2009, Copa America 2021, World Cup 2022.

prompt: Which club did Sergio Aguero score a title-winning goal for in the dying seconds of the 2011-12 Premier League season?
explanation: Aguero's 93rd-minute goal won Manchester City their first Premier League title.

prompt: Who scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup Final for France?
explanation: Mbappe scored a hat-trick, but Argentina still won the final on penalties.

prompt: Which club won the Europa League and Champions League double-check-era rival to Real Madrid dominance, winning it in 2021 under Tuchel having taken over mid-season?
explanation: Chelsea won the 2021 Champions League under Thomas Tuchel, who took charge mid-season.

prompt: Who was appointed Chelsea manager mid-season in 2021 and led them to Champions League glory?
explanation: Thomas Tuchel replaced Frank Lampard and led Chelsea to the 2021 Champions League title.

prompt: Which player scored the fastest hat-trick in Champions League history for Bayern Munich against Barcelona in 2020?
explanation: Bayern thrashed Barcelona 8-2 in that 2020 quarter-final, with Lewandowski among the scorers.

prompt: What was the score when Bayern Munich thrashed Barcelona in the 2020 Champions League quarter-final?
explanation: Bayern demolished Barcelona 8-2 in Lisbon, one of the biggest shocks in the competition's history.

prompt: Which country did Robert Lewandowski represent internationally?
explanation: Lewandowski is Poland's all-time leading goal scorer.

prompt: Which club did Sadio Mane help win the Champions League in 2019?
explanation: Mane was part of the front three that helped Liverpool win the 2019 Champions League.

prompt: Who managed Real Madrid to their 14th and 15th Champions League titles (2022 and 2024)?
explanation: Carlo Ancelotti led Real Madrid to Champions League titles in 2022 and 2024.

prompt: Which player scored twice in the 2022 Champions League Final for Real Madrid... wait, who scored the winner in the 2022 final?
explanation: Vinicius Junior scored the only goal as Real Madrid beat Liverpool 1-0 in 2022.

prompt: Which African nation reached the semi-finals of the 2022 FIFA World Cup, a first for the continent?
explanation: Morocco became the first African nation to reach a World Cup semi-final in 2022.

prompt: Who eliminated Portugal in the round of 16 at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Morocco beat Portugal 1-0 to reach the quarter-finals of the 2022 World Cup.

prompt: Which club did N'Golo Kante help win both the Premier League (with Leicester and Chelsea) and Champions League (with Chelsea)?
explanation: Kante won the Premier League with Leicester (2016) and Chelsea (2017), and the Champions League with Chelsea (2021).

prompt: Which stadium hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup Final?
explanation: The 2022 final between Argentina and France was played at Lusail Stadium.

prompt: Who was the top scorer of Euro 2024?
explanation: Euro 2024's Golden Boot was shared among several players tied on three goals each.

prompt: Which club knocked Manchester City out of the 2023-24 Champions League?
explanation: Real Madrid eliminated defending champions Manchester City on penalties in the quarter-finals.

prompt: Which club won the UEFA Europa League in 2023?
explanation: Sevilla won a record seventh Europa League title in 2023, beating Roma on penalties.

prompt: Which player captained England at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Harry Kane captained England, who reached the quarter-finals of the 2022 World Cup.

prompt: Which team eliminated England from the 2022 World Cup in the quarter-finals?
explanation: France beat England 2-1 in the quarter-finals of the 2022 World Cup.

prompt: Who won the 2022-23 Ballon d'Or Feminin race — which player finished as runner-up to Aitana Bonmati?
explanation: Alexia Putellas, the previous winner, finished as a runner-up as Bonmati won in 2023.

prompt: Which manager led Napoli to their first Serie A title in over 30 years in 2023?
explanation: Luciano Spalletti guided Napoli to a dominant Serie A title win in the 2022-23 season.

prompt: Who scored twice in the 2023 Champions League Final for Manchester City... actually who scored City's only goal?
explanation: Rodri scored the only goal as Manchester City beat Inter Milan 1-0 in the 2023 final.

prompt: Which club did Erling Haaland break the Premier League single-season goal record with in 2022-23?
explanation: Haaland scored 36 Premier League goals in his debut season for Manchester City.

prompt: Which country won the inaugural UEFA Nations League in 2019?
explanation: Portugal beat the Netherlands 1-0 in the final on home soil in 2019.

prompt: Who scored the winning goal for Portugal in the 2019 UEFA Nations League final?
explanation: Goncalo Guedes scored the only goal as Portugal won the first Nations League title.

prompt: Which club won the 2021-22 Premier League title on the final day with a dramatic comeback against Aston Villa?
explanation: Manchester City came back from 2-0 down to beat Aston Villa 3-2 and win the title.

prompt: Who scored a hat-trick in the final minutes to win the title for Manchester City against Aston Villa in 2022?
explanation: Ilkay Gundogan scored twice in the last five minutes to complete the comeback.

prompt: Which country did Sadio Mane help win their first Africa Cup of Nations title in 2021 (played in 2022)?
explanation: Senegal won their first AFCON title in 2022, beating Egypt on penalties, with Mane scoring the decisive kick.

prompt: Which club is Vinicius Junior most associated with?
explanation: Vinicius Junior joined Real Madrid from Flamengo in 2018 and became a key attacking player.

prompt: Which country won the 2023 FIFA Women's World Cup, held in Australia and New Zealand?
explanation: Spain won their first Women's World Cup title, beating England 1-0 in the final.

prompt: Who scored the winning goal for Spain in the 2023 Women's World Cup final?
explanation: Olga Carmona scored the only goal as Spain beat England 1-0 in the final.

prompt: Which player won the Premier League Golden Boot for the 2023-24 season?
explanation: Erling Haaland topped the scoring charts again in the 2023-24 Premier League season.

prompt: Which club won the 2022-23 UEFA Europa Conference League?
explanation: West Ham beat Fiorentina 2-1 to win their first European trophy since 1965.

statement: Morocco became the first African nation to reach a FIFA World Cup semi-final in 2022.
explanation: Morocco's historic run at the 2022 World Cup ended in the semi-finals against France.

statement: Manchester City won the Premier League title on the final day of the 2021-22 season.
explanation: City came from 2-0 down to beat Aston Villa 3-2 and clinch the title.

statement: Sevilla has won more UEFA Europa League titles than any other club.
explanation: Sevilla have won a record seven Europa League/UEFA Cup titles.
